rulings.Case Title: COLE ASHBURY GROUP, LLC VS. COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2025, Line 1. PETITIONER COLE ASHBURY GROUP, LLC's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Entry Of Judgment. Petitioner Cole Ashbury Group, LLC's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Respondent Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. failed to show that the final award should be vacated under any of the limited grounds for which an arbitration award may be vacated. Other than the statements of Cookies' counsel, there is no evidence that Judge Garcia made a commitment that any significant changes to his interim award would be reflected in another interim award rather than a final award and there is substantial evidence to the contrary. Thus Cookies has not met its burden of showing that the asserted commitment was made. Moreover, even if Judge Garcia made such a commitment, his failure to comply with that commitment is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award where, as here, Cookies had a full opportunity to oppose in writing all arguments made by CAG in it objections to the interim award prior to the date that Judge Garcia assertedly made his commitment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To For The Reconsideration Vacate Elvia Juarez' Renewal Of The Judgment, The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And The Statements On The Oath
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANTS EDWARD NOVAK INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE EXECUTOR AND ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF RAISSA NOVAK, AND ANTHONY NOVAK's Motion To For The Reconsideration Vacate Elvia Juarez' Renewal Of The Judgment. Defendant Edward Novak's motion for reconsideration of the March 18, 2025 order is denied. Mr. Novak has not shown any new or different facts, law or circumstances warranting reconsideration nor does the court believe that the March 18, 2025 order should be reconsidered based on the court's inherent authority to reconsider rulings. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. MICHAEL B. ROTHENBERG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Michael B. Rothenberg For Failure To Comply With Orders Compelling Discovery Responses And Deposition And For An Order Allowing Entry Of Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, May 29, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFFS FOLSOM STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC, AND CRAIG HORDLOW's Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Michael B. Rothenberg For Failure To Comply With Orders Compelling Discovery Responses And Deposition And For An Order Allowing Entry Of Default Judgment. Plaintiffs' motion for terminating and monetary sanctions against defendant Michael Rothenberg is denied. This motion is based on the assertion that Mr. Rothenberg has violated several court orders. However, the only evidence submitted by plaintiffs of any violations of any court orders occurred more than three and a half years ago and there is no explanation of why this motion was not made till now nor of what has transpired in the interim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
